purpose: Operator-owned boundary profile. This file is intentionally a template until the operator defines what FATHIYA may do automatically, what requires approval, and what remains disabled.

default_runtime_behavior_while_pending.description: Knowledge, planning, read-only local checks, receipts, and currently configured paper-mode trading may continue. Sensitive or state-changing actions should be labeled boundary_pending until the operator fills this profile.

questions: Which modes are allowed automatically: paper, testnet, broker-connected, or none?
What model authority is allowed: critique, veto, confirm, originate, or another role?
What risk limits, max order sizes, daily loss limits, and receipt requirements apply?
Which assets count as owned or in-scope?
Which tools may run in local labs automatically?
Which live activities require explicit per-task approval or are out of scope?
Which Zapier, n8n, MCP, and connector actions are read-only?
Which actions may write, publish, notify, mutate accounts, or trigger workflows?
What confirmation and receipt evidence is required?
Which agents may use which credentials?
What account actions require operator confirmation?
Which paths are owned workspace paths?
Which file edits may be automatic?
Which deletes, moves, branch operations, commits, pushes, or deployments require confirmation?
Which channels may FATHIYA draft for?
Which channels may FATHIYA publish to?
What review, approval, and receipt are required?

notes: This template does not grant new permissions by itself.
Agents should not invent operator preferences when operator_decision is null.
When a request hits an undefined boundary, the correct project status is boundary_pending.
